Article.ArticleTitle: Comment on "Aerobic Exercise Preconditioning Does Not Augment Muscle Hypertrophy During Subsequent Resistance Exercise Training in Healthy Older Adults".

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Medical Examination Center, Peking University Third Hospital, Beijing, China.
Sports Coaching College, Beijing Sport University, Beijing, China.
Medical Examination Center, Peking University Third Hospital, Beijing, China. baodp@outlook.com.
China Institute of Sport and Health Science, Beijing Sport University, No. 48 Xinxi Road, Haidian District, Beijing, 100084, China. baodp@outlook.com.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Conflict of Interest: Renjie Bing, Jianyu Gan, and Dapeng Bao have no conflicts of interest that are directly relevant to the content of this letter. Data Availability: No datasets were generated or analyzed during the current study. Author Contributions: RB, JG, and DB wrote this letter. All authors read and approved the final version of the letter.

Reference.Citation: Betz MW, Monsegue AP, Houben LHP, Hendriks FK, Van Kranenburg J, Aussieker T, et al. Aerobic exercise preconditioning does not augment muscle hypertrophy during subsequent resistance exercise training in healthy older adults. Sports Med. 2025;55(9):2323–38.
Kim PL, Staron RS, Phillips SM. Fasted-state skeletal muscle protein synthesis after resistance exercise is altered with training. J Physiol. 2005;568:283–90.

Ogasawara R, Kobayashi K, Tsutaki A, Lee K, Abe T, Fujita S, et al. mTOR signaling response to resistance exercise is altered by chronic resistance training and detraining in skeletal muscle. J Appl Physiol (1985). 2013;114:934–40.
Moberg M, Lindholm ME, Reitzner SM, Ekblom B, Sundberg C-J, Psilander N. Exercise induces different molecular responses in trained and untrained human muscle. Med Sci Sports Exerc. 2020;52:1679–90.
Bagley JR, Burghardt KJ, McManus R, Howlett B, Costa PB, Coburn JW, et al. Epigenetic responses to acute resistance exercise in trained vs. sedentary men. J Strength Cond Res. 2020;34:1574–80.
Phillips SM, Tipton KD, Ferrando AA, Wolfe RR. Resistance training reduces the acute exercise-induced increase in muscle protein turnover. Am J Physiol. 1999;276:E118–24.

Wilkinson SB, Phillips SM, Atherton PJ, Patel R, Yarasheski KE, Tarnopolsky MA, et al. Differential effects of resistance and endurance exercise in the fed state on signalling molecule phosphorylation and protein synthesis in human muscle. J Physiol. 2008;586:3701–17.

Phillips SM, Parise G, Roy BD, Tipton KD, Wolfe RR, Tamopolsky MA. Resistance-training-induced adaptations in skeletal muscle protein turnover in the fed state. Can J Physiol Pharmacol. 2002;80:1045–53.
